overview: Izzet Terror is a control-oriented Pauper deck that leverages cheap removal, countermagic, and cantrips to stabilize against aggressive and combo strategies. It utilizes meta-call cards like Cast into the Fire to specifically target top-tier decks such as Affinity and Kuldotha Red. Once the opponent's resources are depleted, the deck closes games with resilient threats like Tolarian Terror or token generation from Murmuring Mystic.

keep_criteria: Hands containing 2-3 lands to avoid mana screw
Early interaction like Counterspell or cheap removal
Cantrips to dig for necessary answers
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one early interaction (Counterspell, Spell Pierce, Lightning Strike, or Abrade), and Lorien Revealed if you have 3+ mana sources.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking lands or early interaction
Hands heavy on late-game threats without draw or protection
Mulligan on 1-land hands, hands with only removal and no cantrips/interaction, or hands with multiple Lorien Revealed but no early spells.

examples.reason: Provides early interaction, removal, and card advantage to stabilize against early pressure.

early_game: Play lands and use cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder to find answers
Deploy cheap removal and Counterspell to disrupt opponent's early threats
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Opt/Ponder to dig for interaction or Lorien.
Turn 2: Play a second land, hold up 2-3 mana for counterspells against key threats.
If opponent plays a vulnerable early creature, remove it or counter it.
If you have 3 mana, cast Lorien Revealed to start generating tokens and drawing cards.
mid_game: Stabilize the board using sweepers like Breath Weapon and meta calls like Cast into the Fire
Maintain Monarch state if possible to generate extra card advantage
Preserve counterspells for game-ending threats or combo pieces
Don’t overcommit to Lorien if you have no way to protect it or follow up.
Commit Lorien tokens when you have board presence or can protect them with counterspells.
Hold back on Skred until the opponent has no removal or can’t block effectively.
late_game: Deploy Tolarian Terror to create a four-turn clock or endless blockers
Use Murmuring Mystic to flood the board with tokens once opponent's resources are exhausted
Skred (often pumped via Lightning Strike or similar effects) or Lorien Revealed token army.
Secure victory by clearing the board of blockers, then attacking with Skred or tokens.
Go all-in when the opponent has no removal, counterspells, or board wipes in hand, and you have a card advantage advantage.
Commit to Skred attacks when you can safely pump it or they have no blockers.

key_cards.name: Tolarian Terror
key_cards.notes: Main threat that protects itself and creates a four-turn clock or endless stream of blockers.

key_cards.name: Cast into the Fire
key_cards.notes: Targets Affinity's Modern Horizons 2 lands, clears Faeries tokens, and handles Kuldotha Rebirth tokens.

key_cards.name: Counterspell
key_cards.notes: Classic Pauper blue card used to secure early game and answer opponent's key spells.

key_cards.name: Brainstorm
key_cards.notes: Digs deeper into the deck to find necessary answers and threats.

key_cards.name: Ponder
key_cards.notes: Helps shuffle useless cards on top and smooths out the draw step.

key_cards.name: Galvanic Discharge
key_cards.notes: Handles multiple creatures like Myr Enforcer, Tolarian Terror, and Writhing Chrysalis.

key_cards.name: Skred
key_cards.notes: Best red removal against most creatures, though slow against early 2-toughness aggro.

key_cards.name: Breath Weapon
key_cards.notes: Primary answer against aggro and go-wide strategies in Pauper.

sequencing_tips: Use Brainstorm to dig deep, then follow up with Ponder to shuffle unfavorable cards on top.
Cast Lorien Revealed to search for Volatile Fjord, ensuring consistent red mana access.
Time Cast into the Fire to clear multiple tokens or lands simultaneously for maximum efficiency.
Retain Monarch by avoiding combat damage to draw an extra card each turn.

Common mistakes include overcommitting to Lorien Revealed without follow-up, holding up too many counterspells and missing early interaction windows, and underestimating Skred’s vulnerability to board wipes.
Always track the opponent’s graveyard and hand size, as Izzet Terror relies heavily on information and timing.
In mirrors, card advantage and counterspell efficiency are paramount—don’t trade spells unnecessarily.

matchups.advice: Sideboard in Hydroblast to handle Kuldotha Rebirth and creatures. Be cautious of early Goblin Tomb Raider plays.
Sideboard in Gorilla Shaman and Annul. Use Cast into the Fire to target Modern Horizons 2 lands and Annul for artifacts/enchantments.
Sideboard in Annul and Dispel to answer artifacts, enchantments, and protective spells like Tamiyo's Safekeeping.
Sideboard in Pyroblast and consider Dispel. Use Pyroblast to bypass counterspells and answer their threats.
Sideboard in Pyroblast and rely on maindeck Cast into the Fire. Clear tokens efficiently and use Pyroblast for key threats.

Use cheap cantrips to dig through counterspells, cast Lorien Revealed on turns when you can protect it, and use Skred as a threat that’s hard to counter. Cast spells in response to their counterspells to force trades.

Apply early pressure with removal and counterspells to disrupt their mana development. Bring in artifact hate and discard to slow them down. Don’t let them stabilize; finish them before they hit their late-game threats.
Prioritize early interaction to stabilize. Use Lorien Revealed to generate blockers and card advantage. Track life total and don’t overextend into board wipes.
Out-value them with Lorien Revealed and card draw. Use Skred as a finisher they can’t easily answer. Force them to use resources on your threats while you dig for answers.

Win the resource war by drawing more cards and holding up counterspells for key plays. Prioritize Lorien Revealed and card draw over aggressive threats. Use Skred when they have no counterspells left. Track their hand size and graveyard to predict their game plan.

common_threats: Watch for combo win conditions, note when opponents tap out for big threats (your counterspell window), and track graveyard activity for reanimation or discard strategies.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Izzet Terror is a more control-oriented variant of Tolarian Terror lists that runs cards like Cast into the Fire to prey on the best Pauper decks

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Tolarian Terror is our main threat to win games.
Murmuring Mystic... start creating an army of tokens with it.

its damage mode also allows you to deal with Faeries and two Kuldotha Rebirth tokens with a single card
Monarch , which generates an extra draw every turn in which we retain this game state by not taking combat damage.
